Student guide · Osmanpura · 2025

Living near Deogiri College — your complete Osmanpura neighbourhood guide

Everything a new student needs to know about daily life in Jyoti Nagar and Osmanpura, Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

Getting oriented — Osmanpura as a student hub

Osmanpura is the most student-dense neighbourhood in Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar. With Deogiri College, DIEMS engineering, and a cluster of NEET and JEE coaching institutes all within a 2 km radius, the area has evolved naturally into a hub for students from across Marathwada — Latur, Nanded, Beed, Osmanabad, and Hingoli.

If you are joining Deogiri College or DIEMS and looking for a PG in Osmanpura, this guide covers everything you need to know about daily life in the neighbourhood — food, transport, medical, banking, and more.

Food options near Deogiri College

Osmanpura has a strong tiffin and canteen culture built around its student population. Most PG residents use a combination of a home tiffin service for lunch and dinner, plus local food stalls for breakfast and quick meals.

  • Home tiffin services — several local tiffin providers deliver directly to PGs in Jyoti Nagar. Typical cost: ₹60–80 per meal, ₹1,800–2,200 per month for two meals daily.
  • Cafe Aveen's — located near Jyoti Girls PG, serves quick meals including Maggi, omelettes, biryani, and beverages. Popular with students.
  • Osmanpura main road stalls — poha, vada pav, chai, and breakfast items available from early morning at low cost.
  • College canteen — both Deogiri College and DIEMS have on-campus canteens for quick meals between lectures.

Auto rickshaws and getting around

Auto rickshaws are the primary mode of transport in Osmanpura. Key routes and approximate fares from Jyoti Nagar:

  • Jyoti Nagar → Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar railway station: ₹60–80, approximately 10–12 minutes
  • Jyoti Nagar → Osmanpura bus stand: ₹20–30, 5 minutes
  • Jyoti Nagar → City Chowk (main market): ₹40–60, 8–10 minutes
  • Jyoti Nagar → DIEMS campus: ₹30–40, 5 minutes (or 10-minute walk)

Most students use shared autos on the main Osmanpura road for daily commuting — cost is ₹10–15 per trip for short distances.

Pharmacies and medical

Medical support is well-covered in the Osmanpura area. Several pharmacies operate within a 500m radius of Jyoti Nagar, most open until 10 PM. Government Medical College and Hospital (GMCH) — the city's main government hospital — is approximately 3 km away. Private clinics and general physicians are available within the neighbourhood for routine consultations.

ATMs and banking

Multiple ATMs are available on the Osmanpura main road within 5 minutes' walk — SBI, Bank of Maharashtra, and HDFC branches are all represented in the area. For most day-to-day transactions, UPI and QR-based payments are widely accepted by local shops, canteens, and tiffin services.

Daily essentials and shopping

Osmanpura market on the main road stocks all daily essentials — stationery, toiletries, clothing, and groceries. For larger purchases, City Chowk and Nirala Bazaar are 10–15 minutes away by auto and offer a wider range of shops. Most students make a weekly or fortnightly trip to the main market for bulk buying.

Internet and mobile networks

Jio and Airtel both have strong 4G and 5G coverage across Osmanpura. Most PGs — including Jyoti Girls PG — provide in-house Wi-Fi, so a separate SIM data plan is mainly needed for when you are out. Local mobile shops for SIM cards and accessories are available on the main Osmanpura road.

Safety and local environment

Jyoti Nagar is a residential colony — quieter than the main Osmanpura road, well-lit at night, and predominantly inhabited by families and students. The area is considered safe for female students and has a visible local community presence. Most PGs in the area operate with curfew policies and warden supervision for additional safety.

Looking for a girls PG in Osmanpura?

Jyoti Girls PG is 0.8 km from Deogiri College and 0.9 km from DIEMS — from ₹2,999/month.
